TASK: My laptop (order #ORD-11234, SKU: LP-PRO-16, $2,899) arrived with a cracked screen and dented chassis. I need a replacement for a conference presentation this Friday (4 days away). The laptop INSIDE had the cracked screen and dent, which looks like it could be a manufacturing defect rather than shipping damage, (2) I took 14 photos documenting the unboxing, but the shipping carrier (FedEx) says I signed for the package without noting damage (because the outer box was fine), potentially complicating an insurance claim, (3) Your return policy says 'report damage within 48 hours' — I received it Tuesday but didn't open it until today (Thursday) because I was traveling. Am I outside your damage reporting window?, (4) The LP-PRO-16 is now showing as 'backordered, 3-4 weeks' on your website, meaning overnight replacement may not be possible, (5) I need either: overnight replacement (if in stock), a comparable or better loaner unit shipped overnight until permanent replacement arrives, or a full refund so I can buy from another vendor in time for Friday. I'm willing to return the damaged unit at my convenience if you ship the replacement first.
